body.title: METRIC memories (routing_signal, etc.) still deposit without deposited_by
body.result: Stamped observer.observe() with source='runtime:observer' + deposited_by — covers METRIC|routing_signal|speed_pressure (the one Akien saw). Also stamped the interaction-outcome metric in main.py. Commit 2169f297. After Igor restart: PROVENANCE_GAP should be silent on steady-state.
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-19 12:01:53 paste shows: PROVENANCE_GAP gap #1, narrative: METRIC|routing_signal|speed_pressure|reason=quick_followup|...

Yesterday's T-seed-memories-deposited-by fix (cd624a7d) covered CRED_* and PROC_* builtin seeds, but there are other deposit sites — metric memories produced at runtime — that still don't stamp deposited_by. The gate falls back to 'unknown' and logs the warning.

## Candidate sites
- wild_igor/igor/main.py around line 6412 (_metric_mem = Memory(...)) and 7557, 8460, 8995, 9347, 9471 (self.cortex.store(mem)) — per yesterday's grep
- Any call site where the code constructs a Memory() and passes to cortex.store without setting .source or metadata.deposited_by

## Fix pattern
For each site identified: stamp a source label that identifies the SUBSYSTEM producing the memory (e.g. 'runtime:metric', 'runtime:episodic', 'runtime:pr_accretion'). Same shape as yesterday's seed:credentials / seed:habits labels — terse, subsystem-scoped, audit-useful.

## Acceptance
- No PROVENANCE_GAP warnings at steady-state conversation turn
- Existing metric memories aren't modified (leave the historical 'unknown' alone)

## Related
- T-seed-memories-deposited-by (cd624a7d, done — same pattern, different sites)
- T-reading-audit-qwen-complete (model_used threading — same goal for the reading subsystem)
